rdkit_filter_catalog_support.check_command: from rdkit.Chem import FilterCatalog; inspect FilterCatalogParams.FilterCatalogs

metric_names.name: pains_alert_count
metric_names.coverage_semantics: available when RDKit sanitization succeeds and FilterCatalog PAINS_A/B/C can be constructed

metric_names.name: pains_free_fraction
metric_names.coverage_semantics: aggregate fraction of candidates with zero PAINS alerts among filter-scored candidates

metric_names.name: brenk_alert_count
metric_names.coverage_semantics: available when RDKit sanitization succeeds and FilterCatalog BRENK can be constructed

metric_names.name: brenk_free_fraction
metric_names.coverage_semantics: aggregate fraction of candidates with zero Brenk alerts among filter-scored candidates

metric_names.name: medchem_filter_coverage_fraction
metric_names.coverage_semantics: filter-scored candidate count divided by backend input count

integration_plan.implementation: Build optional RDKit FilterCatalogs after RDKit import; emit per-candidate alert counts and aggregate free fractions.
integration_plan.failure_behavior: If FilterCatalog import or construction fails, emit medchem_filter_coverage_fraction=0 and omit alert counts; Q2 core evaluation must not fail.
integration_plan.table_rendering: When unsupported or uncomputed, render PAINS/Brenk metrics as NA, not zero.

claim_guardrails: PAINS/Brenk are screening filters, not proof of developability.
Unavailable filter metrics must not block Vina/GNINA/RDKit core Q2 tables.
Do not compare methods on PAINS/Brenk unless filter coverage is reported.
